name: Very Hot Cajun Sauce for Beef
recipeInstructions:
This sauce pairs well with Cajun Meat Loaf, roast beef sandwiches, hamburgers, pot roast, and Cajun Shepherd's Pie.
Note: Fresh jalapenos are preferred; if using pickled ones, rinse as much vinegar off them as possible.
1. Combine the chopped onion, green bell pepper, and celery in a small bowl and set aside while you prepare the roux.
2. Heat vegetable oil over medium-low heat to 250°F (medium-low). Whisk in flour gradually until smooth, cooking for about 2-3 minutes or until light brown. Avoid scorching or splashing the mixture on your skin.
3. Remove from heat and stir in the vegetable mixture and peppers, returning the pan to high heat. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
4. Add bay leaves, jalapeno pepper, and garlic; stir well. Cook for another 2 minutes (the mixture should be pasty).
5. In a separate saucepan, bring beef stock to a boil. Gradually add the roux mixture, stirring until dissolved between each addition. Return to boiling, then reduce heat to a simmer.
6. Simmer until the sauce reduces to about 3 1/2 cups (about 15 minutes). Skim any oil from the surface and serve immediately.
